title: Blue Depths
description: This is a datapack that improves the Minecraft ocean and river ecosystem by adding new mobs, blocks, and a brand new ocean boss. The new sea creatures include Orcas, Sharks, Sturgeons, Jellyfish, Catfish, and Tuna
body:
![Blue Depths RR Part 1 Banner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9665504ec8b62d55d442853d31d476334f1f49c2_0.webp)

[[Latest Resource Pack Download](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/h1VZfZYb/versions/PIxdbiYB/blue_depths%20Resourcepack%20v1.1.0.zip?mr_download_reason=standalone)]

[[Check out the Planet Minecraft Page](https://www.planetminecraft.com/data-pack/blue-depths-ocean-ecosystem-expansion-datapack-sharks-jellyfish-boss-1-21-9/)]

[[Give Feedback and Suggestions](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd_3-NI__9EVzw390CbgHJvgELaNKstVcrfBkY-t7P1QB0rFQ/viewform?usp=publish-editor)]

**[The next content update for Blue Depths will be part 2 of Roaring Rivers, the final part of Roaring Rivers. The update after Roaring Rivers will focus on a different theme (TBA)]**

Blue Depths transforms Minecraft’s oceans AND rivers into a living, breathing ecosystem, providing new creatures that Minecraft normally wouldn’t add.

Blue Depths is meant for players who want a deeper ocean experience without sacrificing performance or vanilla balancing. Players can conquer these new creatures and even raise them as their own, experiencing their life cycles.

Along with that, the datapack also adds a new difficult boss to the game, The Nameless Depth, a being lost to time and forgotten by the ocean.

An in-game guidebook can be crafted using a book and salmon, and provides a much better explanation of all the features.

## Compatibility
- Full compatible with multiplayer
- Datapacks
  - Made compatible with [Deep Blue: Ocean Survival](https://modrinth.com/datapack/deep-blue-ocean-survival) by it's creator [FunkyToc](https://modrinth.com/user/FunkyToc)
  - Should be compatible with most other datapacks as well.

# What Blue Depths adds:

## Mobs:
### NEW: Largemouth Bass
- Exclusively spawns in rivers
- Brand new source of food
- Adds variety to river ecosystems
### NEW: Archerfish
- River exclusive fish
- Has a unique ability: Spitting water at prey and players!
- Attacks spiders, bees, and other insects with it's water spit
### NEW: Bull Shark
- Spawns in lukewarm oceans and rivers
- Territorial and more aggressive than Great White Sharks
- If defeated, can drop a pup that can be raised and grown by the player
### Tuna (inspired by the Atlantic Bluefin Tuna)
- The “cow of the sea” (a new food source)
- Spawns in schools of four in most ocean biomes
- Can be grown and harvested for farms
### Great White Shark
- Neutral predator in most cases (unless provoked)
  - High health and damage
- Rare, curious variant will follow and attack players
- If defeated, it can drop a pup that can be raised and grown by the player
### Box Jellyfish
- If you get too close, it will inflict a nasty sting that will debuff and harm the player
- Multiple colored variants
  - An extremely rare pink variant can also be found in the wild
- Drops the Nematocyst Gel
  - Can be processed into slime balls
- Can be grown by the player from a polyp to a mature medusa
### Orcas
- Spawn in pairs
- Inspired by real social behaviors
- Planned improvements in the future
### Catfish
- Spawns in rivers and mangrove swamps
- Bottom-dwelling fish
- Can be picked up using a water bucket
- Another source of food
### Sturgeon
- Rare and durable river fish
- Has a chance to drop their scute on death
  - Scute can be processed to make the Armorbite, which can give players resistance for 3 minutes
- Can drop eggs, which can be used to raise and grow more sturgeon

## Blocks:
### Deep Blue Grill
- A custom block used for cooking and processing certain drops from the new mobs
- Can be used to cook tuna fillet and catfish
- Also used for processing nematocyst gel to slime balls and sturgeon scute
### The Nameless Catalyst
- A new ritual block used to summon the boss, an entity forgotten by time and hidden away by the ocean
- Place certain drops from each of the new mobs to activate it
  - Certain restrictions apply

## Boss:
### The Nameless Depth
- A new underwater boss that rivals most Minecraft bosses in difficulty
- Launches custom attacks, causing chaos on the battlefield
- Forces player submergence
- Scales stats and attack frequency based on player count
- Upon death, it will drop a new powerful weapon for the player(s) to wield

# To begin your journey:
Install the datapack along with its REQUIRED resourcepack, make sure the resourcepack is enabled
Craft the in-game guidebook for a comprehensive guide on Blue Depths.

Also, there is an easter egg for players to discover.
